title: Context Propagation, Deadlines, OS Signals, and Graceful Shutdown · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers context propagation, deadlines, os signals, and graceful shutdown into a changed signal-aware graceful command lifecycle. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of signal-aware graceful command lifecycle with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Create one root context with signal.NotifyContext, call its stop function, and define portable interrupt behavior.
Propagate caller context through session, handler, loader, transport, cache wait, and persistence without storing it in long-lived structs.
Reserve cleanup time, derive bounded operation deadlines, and distinguish parent cancellation from local timeout.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Root Signal Context
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 8147 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove root signal cancellation, context propagation, worker completion, and idempotent cleanup; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove root signal cancellation, context propagation, worker completion, and idempotent cleanup; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test cancel before, during, and after work; second interrupt policy; state preservation; output; goroutine completion; and idempotent cleanup.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
